page: OVHcloud b3-64 is a general-purpose cloud server powered by an AMD EPYC processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs operating at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz without hyperthreading. The hardware configuration includes 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB per core. Local storage is provided by a 400 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance is provisioned with a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps and one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores placing in the top 25% of tested instances. Memory performance is also a key strength, showing strong results in large-block read and write bandwidth tests, alongside a high PassMark memory score. Multi-core performance, including stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core metrics, falls within the average range. The server demonstrates strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and text processing capabilities, while database operations, static web serving, and software compilation perform at average levels.

Given its dedicated CPU allocation and balanced memory-to-core ratio, the b3-64 offers a reliable resource profile for general-purpose workloads. It is well-suited for applications requiring consistent CPU performance without hyperthreading overhead, such as text processing, medium-scale web serving, and memory-bound tasks. The inclusion of local NVMe storage and dedicated resources provides a cost-efficient option for workloads that demand steady-state performance without the risk of resource contention common in shared-core environments.
description: OVHcloud b3-64 is a general-purpose virtual server built on the x86_64 architecture and powered by an AMD EPYC processor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs running at 2.3 GHz, with no hyperthreading. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, a 400 GB NVMe SSD, and a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps. Benchmark results show strong single-core CPU and large-block memory bandwidth performance, while multi-core execution and database operations remain in the average tier. This server is designed for workloads requiring dedicated CPU allocation and balanced memory resources, such as text processing, software compilation, medium-scale web serving, and general-purpose application hosting.
og_description: OVHcloud b3-64 is a general-purpose server featuring 16 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 64 GB RAM, and a 400 GB NVMe SSD, delivering strong single-core and memory performance for balanced enterprise workloads.
meta_description: OVHcloud b3-64 is a general-purpose server with 16 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 64 GB RAM, and 400 GB NVMe SSD storage for balanced workloads.
tagline: A dedicated sixteen-core AMD EPYC server with NVMe storage, offering balanced memory and strong single-core performance for general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated AMD EPYC physical cores with no hyperthreading
64 GB of system memory and 400 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Strong single-core CPU and large-block memory bandwidth benchmarks
4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with one public IPv4 address
Suitable for text processing, software compilation, and web serving
